sections.title: Authority
sections.body: This manual implements Section 300 and Section 310 of the state minimum coverage statute.

sections.title: Required Action
sections.body:
When a claim is denied for insufficient coverage, the adjuster shall send notice within 10 business days.
The notice workflow references Section 30.

sections.title: Evidence
sections.body: The claim file must include policy version, denial reason, notice date, and reviewer initials.

sections.title: External Guidance
sections.body: The claims team should consult NAIC guidance and ISO circulars when updating templates.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: This chapter establishes minimum coverage rules for private passenger auto policies.

sections.title: Definitions
sections.body:
- "Covered vehicle" means a vehicle insured under a policy subject to this chapter.
- "Legacy endorsement" means an endorsement form retired before January 1, 2020.
- "Policy year" means the annual period beginning on the policy effective date.

sections.title: Minimum Coverage
sections.body:
An insurer shall provide minimum liability coverage as described in Section 310.
Notices required by this section must comply with Section 900.

sections.title: Coverage Amounts
sections.body:
Coverage amounts shall follow NAIC Model Law guidance unless a more specific state rule applies.
The disclosure template must follow ISO form language.

sections.title: Claim Notice
sections.body: Claim notice timing is governed by Section 410.

sections.title: Claim Notice Exception
sections.body: Exceptions to claim notice timing are governed by Section 400.
